label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out step by step. So the question is whether following this set of instructions will bring me back to the starting point. The options are yes or no. Alright, let's break down the instructions one by one and track my position and direction. First, the starting point is where I am initially. Let's assume I'm facing north. Sometimes, these problems assume you start facing a specific direction, like north, unless told otherwise. So I'll go with that. 1. Turn right. If I was facing north, turning right would make me face east. So now I'm facing east. 2. Take 9 steps. Since I'm facing east, I'll move 9 steps to the east. Let's note that my position is now (9, 0) if we consider the starting point as (0, 0), with east as the positive x-axis and north as the positive y-axis. 3. Take 10 steps. Still facing east, so I take another 10 steps. Adding to my previous position: 9 + 10 = 19 steps east. Now I'm at (19, 0). 4. Turn left. Facing east, turning left would make me face north now. Because left from east is north. So now direction is north. 5. Take 10 steps. Moving north from (19, 0) by 10 steps. New position is (19, 10). 6. Turn right. Facing north, turning right would make me face east. Now direction is east again. 7. Turn around. Turning around means turning 180 degrees. So facing east, turning around makes me face west. So now direction is west. 8. Take 4 steps. Moving west from (19, 10) by 4 steps. Subtracting from x-coordinate: 19 -
